description: Protocol for pushing FDS-compliant designs to Figma using MCP. All insertions must use components from the FDS library files. No custom ad-hoc components when a library equivalent exists. Variables must be bound — never hardcoded.

workflow.instruction: Parse the design requirement. List every FDS component needed. For each, look up the correct library lookup JSON (flow-app-v2.lookup.json or flow-web-v2.lookup.json) to get the nodeId, canonical props, and defaultVariant. Also identify which FDS icons are needed (flow-icons-v4.lookup.json).

For each component, call Figma MCP get_screenshot(nodeId, fileKey) to confirm visual context before insertion. This prevents misidentification of variants. If get_screenshot fails, use the netlifyFallback URL from the lookup JSON.
Confirm the target platform. Check references/platform-rules.json. App components must never be pushed to Web files and vice versa. Email components are strictly separate.
Call start-editing-transaction on the TARGET design file (not the library file). Capture the transaction ID for use in subsequent operations.
workflow.note: Never start a transaction on a library file. Target files are project/feature Figma files.

workflow.instruction: Use perform-editing-operations to insert components by their library nodeId. Reference the exact nodeId from the lookup JSON. Apply defaultVariant props first, then override with the specific variant needed for the design.
rules: Never create a custom frame when a library component exists for the same purpose
Insert sub-components as library instances — never flatten or detach
For icons: always use flow-icons-v4 library — never embed custom SVGs
For Arabic/RTL: set Language=Arabic on the component — do not manually mirror

workflow.instruction: After insertion, set the component props according to the design requirement. Prop names and values must match the canonical values in prop-lookup.json and the component's lookup JSON entry.
propRules: All prop names in Title Case — 'Show Icon' not 'show icon'
All prop values in Title Case — 'Default' not 'default'
Boolean props: True/False only — never On/Off or Yes/No
Arabic content: use (Ar) suffix props — never 'Arabic Title', always 'Title (Ar)'
Validate prop names against prop-lookup.json synonyms table before setting

workflow.instruction: For any custom frames or elements (not library components): bind all fills, strokes, text styles, and effects to FDS Figma variables from the Foundation file. Never use hardcoded color, spacing, or shadow values.

variableBindingRules.fills: Bind to brand color token variable (e.g. FBrandColorBackgroundPrimary)
variableBindingRules.strokes: Bind to brand border color token variable
variableBindingRules.text: Bind text style to FDS shared text style matching the brand type token
variableBindingRules.effects: Bind shadow/blur effect to brand shadow token variable
variableBindingRules.spacing: Auto Layout padding/gap must reference brand space token variables
forbidden: Never bind to base-tier tokens (FBase*) in designs — those are code-only primitives
Never use local styles — always library or shared styles from Foundation file
Never detach a library component to override a variable

workflow.instruction: Before committing, run through the validation checklist below. If any check fails: cancel transaction, fix the issue, restart from step 4.
checklist: All components are library instances from the correct platform library
Props set to canonical values (validated against prop-lookup.json)
Language=Arabic applied on components that need RTL layout — not manually mirrored
No hardcoded fills or strokes — all bound to Foundation variables
Icons are from flow-icons-v4 library at correct size (24px nav, 48px badge)
Spacing is token-bound in Auto Layout
No components cross-used between platforms (App in Web file or vice versa)

workflow.instruction: Validation passed: call commit-editing-transaction. Any failures: call cancel-editing-transaction, report violations clearly (with component name, prop, and what was wrong), and fix before retrying.
workflow.onSuccess: commit-editing-transaction with the captured transaction ID
workflow.onFailure: cancel-editing-transaction, then log: component name + violation type + corrective action needed

componentInsertionRules.libraryFirst: If a component exists in the FDS library, always insert it from the library. Never build a custom equivalent from primitives.
componentInsertionRules.correctLibraryFile: App designs → flow-app-v2. Web designs → flow-web-v2. Email → flow-email. Never mix.
componentInsertionRules.defaultVariantFirst: Always start from the defaultVariant in the component lookup JSON, then apply the specific Type/State/Size needed.
componentInsertionRules.arabicComponents: Set Language=Arabic prop. Do not manually flip, mirror, or rotate components for RTL.
componentInsertionRules.sizeRestriction: Never override the intrinsic width/height of a library component. Use Auto Layout and constraints for placement.
componentInsertionRules.nestedComponents: Sub-components within a parent must also be library instances. Example: Button inside a Card must be the library Button, not a custom rectangle with text.
componentInsertionRules.iconSource: Icons must always come from flow-icons-v4. Use the exact icon name from the lookup JSON. ic_nav_ at 24px, ic_badge_ at 48px.
componentInsertionRules.stateManagement: Variants represent States — do not manually modify a Default component to look like a Hover/Pressed state. Switch to the correct State variant instead.

figmaVariableCollections.note: Always bind to Brand-tier tokens (FBrand*), not Base-tier (FBase*). Base tokens are raw primitives only used to compose brand tokens.

beforeEveryCommit: All components are library instances (check: no custom group/frame where a component exists)
Platform matches: App components in App designs, Web components in Web designs
Props use canonical names and values (Title Case, True/False booleans)
Arabic: Language=Arabic set on RTL components — not manual mirroring
Icons: from flow-icons-v4 library at 24px (nav) or 48px (badge)
Spacing: Auto Layout padding/gap uses space variables — no fixed numeric values

errorRecovery.onTransactionFailure: Call cancel-editing-transaction immediately. Do not leave a stale transaction open.
errorRecovery.onValidationFailure: List every failing check before cancelling. Fix all issues before restarting transaction.
errorRecovery.onMissingComponent: If a required component is not in the library lookup JSON: flag it as an FDS gap, do not create a custom substitute without flagging it explicitly to the user.
errorRecovery.onMissingIcon: If the required icon does not exist in flow-icons-v4: flag as 'Icon gap: [description] — recommend raising with DS team'. Never embed a custom SVG silently.
